title: cerastium fontanum subsp. membranaceum
intro: Cerastium fontanum subsp. membranaceum, also known as the membranous mouse-ear chickweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Cerastium fontanum subsp. membranaceum include its potential to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and act as a natural remedy for respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used externally or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
